failure_analysis: Active failure modes: high_reward_low_progress and repeated_event_exploitation. The dominant component r_contact is an event_bonus giving 20.0 per leg contact, encouraging the agent to bounce multiple times (8 toggles) for excess reward instead of landing once. shaping_goal_mismatch also flagged because dense penalties for distance/velocity are not guiding a final landing under this exploitation.
memory_usage: No retrieved memory cards were found, so memory did not influence the edit.
operator_reasoning: convert_to_one_time_event is valid for event_bonus components (according to editing principles). Applying it to r_contact will cap the bonus to at most one award per episode, directly countering repeated event exploitation while keeping the sparse landing signal.
diagnosis: Repeated leg contact exploitation inflates episode reward without task progress. One-time conversion of the contact bonus removes the exploit and aligns the reward with a single successful landing.
